notes: Post-fix rerun of Arm B: Salva ran cleanly (retrieval_health=ok, no OMLX errors of note) but still returned qualified_count=0 and zero entities for objective find_partnership_signals — the scoring-layer fix did not resolve the zero-entity issue for this task. Answer was sourced entirely via 2 WebSearch queries against CNCF's own June 21, 2015 launch announcement (cncf.io) and Wikipedia's founding-organizations list. Two tiers reported: (1) the 13 companies CNCF's own press release names as founding members (Google, CoreOS, Mesosphere, Red Hat, Twitter, Huawei, Intel, RX-M, Cisco, IBM, Docker, Univa, VMware); (2) 10 additional organizations Wikipedia lists as part of the broader founding group (AT&T, Box, Cloud Foundry Foundation, Cycle Computing, eBay, Goldman Sachs, Joyent, Kismatic, Switch SUPERNAP, Weaveworks). Note some sources conflate this June 2015 launch list with the much larger December 2015 'new members' announcement (45+ orgs) which is a separate, later membership expansion, not the founding roster.
